response.justification: Strong senior generalist with 15+ years across backend, full-stack, and embedded work, and recent exposure to AI-agent infrastructure and MCP at re:cinq. However, the resume shows only about a year of explicit GenAI work versus the 8+ years required, no Python listed among skills, and no demonstrated experience with RAG, vector databases, or multi-agent frameworks. Worth a screening call to probe depth of recent AI work, but not an obvious staff-level AI hire on paper.
strengths: Recent AI-agent and MCP/code-generator work at re:cinq aligned to JD's agentic-workflow focus
Long track record at staff/senior level with TypeScript, Node.js, and AWS CDK on production systems
Customer-facing experience: workshops, talks, mentoring, and cross-team technical leadership
concerns: Only ~1 year of explicit GenAI experience versus 8+ years required for the role
Python not listed in skills; AI ecosystem is heavily Python-centric
No evidence of RAG, vector databases, LLM evaluation pipelines, or multi-agent frameworks (LangGraph/CrewAI)

key_factors.factor: GenAI experience depth vs 8+ year requirement
Missing core AI stack (Python, RAG, vector DBs, multi-agent frameworks)
Senior engineering breadth and recent MCP/agentic work
